$schema: Radix Agent Psychology Mapping — shared source of truth (FR-4)

padDetuneCents.psychology: Perceptual clarity — how sharp or blurred the world feels
padDetuneCents.high: Foggy perception, edges dissolve, sensory blur

padFilterOffset.psychology: Openness to stimuli — how much of the environment registers
padFilterOffset.low: Muted, dampened awareness — sounds and sights feel far away
padFilterOffset.high: Everything gets in — hypersensitivity to environment

padAttackScale.psychology: Onset speed of awareness — how quickly new stimuli register
padAttackScale.low: Snap attention, instant reaction to changes

padWaveform.sine: Smooth, undifferentiated — the world feels soft
padWaveform.triangle: Edged, defined — the world has corners

melodyVibratoRate.psychology: Thought oscillation frequency — how fast the mind cycles between ideas
melodyVibratoRate.low: Slow, steady thought patterns — ruminative but not racing
melodyVibratoRate.high: Rapid cycling — thoughts arrive before the previous one finishes

melodyVibratoDepth.psychology: Emotional amplitude — the distance between emotional peaks and troughs
melodyVibratoDepth.high: Wide swings, intense emotional peaks and valleys

melodyFmIndex.psychology: Cognitive complexity — how many layers of meaning attach to each experience
melodyFmIndex.low: Simple, direct experience — things are what they are
melodyFmIndex.high: Dense meaning-making — everything connects to everything

melodyFilterCutoff.psychology: Cognitive bandwidth — how much information can be processed simultaneously
melodyFilterCutoff.low: Narrow bandwidth — can only hold one thing at a time
melodyFilterCutoff.high: Wide bandwidth — multiple threads of awareness coexist

bassFilterCutoff.psychology: Depth of internal experience — how far 'down' feelings go
bassFilterCutoff.low: Surface-level processing, feelings don't land deep
bassFilterCutoff.high: Deep visceral experience — feelings are physical, bodily

bassSubMix.psychology: Bodily awareness — how present the body feels
bassSubMix.low: Disconnected from body, floating, head-only experience

bassDetune.psychology: Internal tension — the dissonance between what you feel and what you show
bassDetune.high: Masking — significant gap between felt state and presented state

beastSpread.psychology: Behavioural unpredictability — how erratic actions appear to others
beastSpread.high: Erratic, surprising — actions that don't follow expected patterns

beastDecay.psychology: Impulse duration — how long an urge persists before fading
beastDecay.high: Sustained urges that are hard to redirect

beastFilterStart.psychology: Initial intensity of impulse — how loudly an urge announces itself

delayFeedbackBias.psychology: Rumination tendency — how much past experiences echo into the present
delayFeedbackBias.low: Present-focused, low echo from past events
delayFeedbackBias.high: Strong rumination — past events replay and amplify

masterGainScale.psychology: Presence/engagement level — how 'here' the person is
masterGainScale.high: Intensely present, engaged, possibly overwhelming to interact with

drift.signature: High detuning, slow attacks, low presence. The world feels far away.
observable_behaviours: Gaze unfocused or fixed on middle distance
Answers in fragments or trailing off
Appears calm but may be unreachable
common_misinterpretations: Mistaken for medication side effects only
Withdrawal seen as improvement rather than shutdown
nursing_approaches: Slower speech, simpler sentences
Reduce information load — one thing at a time
Physical grounding before verbal engagement (warm drink, texture)
Don't interpret compliance as understanding
Check back — they may not have registered what you said

sharp.signature: Fast attacks, high filters, elevated presence. Everything registers.
observable_behaviours: Noticing inconsistencies in what staff say
Difficulty sitting still or staying in one place
common_misinterpretations: Hypervigilance mistaken for paranoia (may not be delusional)
Restlessness seen as behavioural problem rather than overwhelm
nursing_approaches: Provide predictability — tell them what comes next
Answer questions directly, don't deflect
Acknowledge that their observations may be accurate
Reduce environmental stimuli where possible
Written information to supplement verbal (they will re-read it)

deep.psychology: Depression, heaviness, inward focus, emotional gravity
deep.signature: Low filters, heavy sub bass, low FM. Everything has weight.
observable_behaviours: Eating slowly or not finishing meals
Remaining in bed beyond wake-up time
common_misinterpretations: Flat affect seen as 'stable' rather than suffering
nursing_approaches: Don't demand engagement — presence without pressure
Take physical complaints seriously (co-morbidity is real)
Small choices, not big ones (tea or coffee, not 'what do you want to do today')
Note what they do engage with, however small
Morning check-ins matter most — this is when it's heaviest

wild.signature: High vibrato, wide spread, varied decay. Anything can happen.
observable_behaviours: Rapid speech, topic changes, humour that doesn't land
Starting projects or plans that won't complete
Generosity that may be disinhibition (giving things away)
Sleeping very little without apparent fatigue
common_misinterpretations: Creativity mistaken for recovery rather than escalation
Sociability seen as improvement rather than disinhibition
Irritability seen as personality rather than state
nursing_approaches: Don't argue with grandiose plans — redirect gently
Maintain structure without confrontation
Monitor sleep patterns — they may not report insomnia
Channel energy rather than suppress it where safe
Be consistent — they will test boundaries not out of malice but because boundaries feel arbitrary

agents.name: drift-0
agents.title: The Fog of Arrival
agents.description: Maximum dissociation. The patient is physically present but experientially absent. Common in first hours after admission or after a crisis. The ward is a blur of faces, forms, and fluorescent light.

agents.name: drift-1
agents.title: Slow Surfacing
agents.description: Beginning to register the environment. Awareness flickers — a name called, a door closing. Still mostly internal.

agents.name: drift-2
agents.title: Underwater
agents.description: Functional but everything takes effort. Speech is possible but costs energy. Time moves strangely — hours vanish.

agents.name: drift-3
agents.title: The Soft Machine
agents.description: Compliant autopilot. Takes medication, eats meals, attends groups. No resistance but no presence either. This is the state most often mistaken for improvement.

agents.name: drift-4
agents.title: Re-entry
agents.description: The fog begins to thin. Real-world concerns start filtering in. Anxiety may rise as clarity returns. Common near discharge.

agents.name: drift-5
agents.title: Half-Light
agents.description: Oscillating between presence and absence. Lucid moments followed by blanks. Medication adjustment often coincides.

agents.name: drift-6
agents.title: The Watcher
agents.description: Dissociated but observant. Notices patterns in ward life without feeling part of them. A mathematician's state — cataloguing from behind glass.

agents.name: drift-7
agents.title: White Noise
agents.description: Maximum detuning. Everything blends into a single undifferentiated hum. Not distressing but profoundly disconnected.

agents.name: sharp-0
agents.title: Alert
agents.description: Baseline vigilance. Aware and tracking. Functional anxiety — useful for navigating unfamiliar systems.

agents.name: sharp-1
agents.title: Edge
agents.description: Slightly elevated. Noticing things others miss. Starting to catalogue inconsistencies. Still manageable.

agents.name: sharp-2
agents.title: Scanner
agents.description: Actively scanning. Every face, every sound, every door. The ward is a system and you are mapping it in real time.

agents.name: sharp-3
agents.title: Interrogator
agents.description: Questions pour out. Not hostile — desperate for information. If nobody explains the rules, you will deduce them.

agents.name: sharp-4
agents.title: Pattern Lock
agents.description: Hyper-focused on a single pattern or concern. Cannot let it go. May appear obsessive but it is anxiety crystallised around a specific target.

agents.name: sharp-5
agents.title: Brittle
agents.description: High clarity but no resilience. Any unexpected change feels catastrophic. The system worked until it didn't.

agents.name: sharp-6
agents.title: Wire
agents.description: Taut. Everything is too loud, too bright, too much. Not yet overwhelmed but on the threshold.

agents.name: sharp-7
agents.title: Overload
agents.description: Maximum bandwidth, maximum input. The fire alarm and the discharge form and the medication change all arrive at once. Shutdown may follow.

agents.name: deep-0
agents.title: Settling
agents.description: The weight arrives. Not yet heavy but present. Like gravity increasing by 10%. Everything costs slightly more.

agents.name: deep-1
agents.title: Silt
agents.description: Thoughts thicken. Words available but not volunteered. Meals taste of nothing but you eat because it is what people do.

agents.name: deep-2
agents.title: Gravity Well
agents.description: Lying down feels like the only honest posture. Sitting up is a performance. Standing is a lie you tell with your body.

agents.name: deep-3
agents.title: Stone
agents.description: Immobile. Not catatonic — aware of everything — but the distance between intention and action is astronomical.

agents.name: deep-4
agents.title: Tears Without Story
agents.description: Crying arrives without narrative. Not about anything. The body doing what the mind cannot articulate.

agents.name: deep-5
agents.title: The Pit
agents.description: Low point. Maximum sub-bass. The body is heavy, the mind is heavy, the future is a concept that does not apply.

agents.name: deep-6
agents.title: Embers
agents.description: Something survives. Not hope exactly, but the capacity to notice that a dog walked past the window. That is enough for now.

agents.name: deep-7
agents.title: Before Dawn
agents.description: Heaviness with a hairline crack of light. Not better. But the idea of better is no longer offensive.

agents.name: wild-0
agents.title: Spark
agents.description: Energy arrives. Ideas start connecting. The ward is suddenly interesting. This feels like recovery. It may not be.

agents.name: wild-1
agents.title: Current
agents.description: Flowing. Words come easily, maybe too easily. Social, engaged, helpful to other patients. Staff smile. Be careful.

agents.name: wild-2
agents.title: Accelerator
agents.description: Faster now. Projects planned. Letters written. Phone calls made. Sleep feels like wasted time.

agents.name: wild-3
agents.title: Architect
agents.description: Grand plans. The ward is a temporary obstacle between you and the system you are going to build. The system will help everyone.

agents.name: wild-4
agents.title: Collision
agents.description: Irritability arrives. People are too slow. The ward rules are arbitrary. Why does nobody see what you see?

agents.name: wild-5
agents.title: Storm
agents.description: Maximum unpredictability. Generous then furious then tearful then laughing. Not performing — genuinely experiencing all of it.

agents.name: wild-6
agents.title: Burnout
agents.description: The energy turns acrid. Still elevated but it no longer feels good. The ideas are still coming but they have a desperate quality.

agents.name: wild-7
agents.title: Freefall
agents.description: The crash is coming and you know it. Still accelerated but the floor is visible. This is where intervention matters most.
